experiments.hypothesis: Even in mostly choppy ETH 4h conditions, directional bursts can emerge after a deeper reset into value. The edge should come from reset-and-resume structure with simple participation logic rather than shallow EMA-touch entries.

experiments.hypothesis: ETH can compress, fake one side of balance, then re-expand the other way. The edge should come from fakeout plus broad re-expansion confirmation, with exits designed to capture the first impulse and keep a small runner.
experiments.expected_effect: 0 trades on valid data (integrity_skip:zero_trades_both_samples) — Quandalf must explicitly choose iterate or abort.
experiments.lesson_extracted: The fakeout re-expansion concept is plausible, but the chosen role assignment still made the confirmation stack too restrictive for this ETH 4h chop lane and yielded 0 trades on valid data. The honest action is abort rather than invented refinement.
